title: Localization-jump safety stop
_about: Second reusable incident rule (proves the spec layer is not obstacle-only). The robot stopped because its localization transform jumped: a TF discontinuity beyond threshold, followed by a stop event and a velocity halt. The extractor emits the CONTINUOUS per-sample jump magnitude (tf_jump_m); this spec owns the threshold. Frame names live in the topic mapping profile, never here. Same compiler, same unchanged verifier.

tf_jump._note: Per-sample translation discontinuity of the localization transform. Normal motion between consecutive samples is ~v*dt (centimeters); a relocalization jump is an order of magnitude larger. Absent -> placeholder -> low.

conclusions.statement: The robot stopped because its localization transform jumped beyond the safe threshold.

expected._about: Oracle for this spec: a TF jump followed by stop+halt must verify high/ok; the same stop+halt WITHOUT any TF jump must verify low/ok (the jump is required positive evidence, so the conclusion is not confirmable from the stop alone).
